DESCRIPTION: Both doors to the building have problems.   The door on the north side of the building, toward the loop road, sometimes jams and will not open. Swiping my card leads to the audible sound of the lock releasing, but the door still cannot be opened. In these cases, the door feels like it is jammed closed. Exiting the door fixes this problem, so I think it might be closing too tightly. This problem can be generated for testing by firmly pushing against the right hand door (facing the building) from the outside. The door jams and then will not open.   The south door, towards the Cambridge Office Building, has an entirely different issue. After hours, when the door locks, the right hand door (as you are leaving the building) doesn't latch properly if someone leaves. It closes most of the way, but is effectively not locked. If you walk up to it from the south of the building the (now left-hand) door is jammed partly open. A steady pull will open it easily.

WORK_TASK_NAME_ticket: RICE - DEFAULT - Work Task Template - Corrective Maintenance-Cardreader, Not Working  (billable over $2K)
DESCRIPTION: Recently, I've been having issues with my keycard not working. I reported this to Access Control, and they replaced the card. Since then, it hasn't worked at all for access after business hours. I should have access to the facility; this has been confirmed by my supervisor and by RUPD.   With my new card, which has an RFID, if I swipe my card, the red light turns green briefly, but the door does not unlock and it quickly returns to red. If I tap the card, the same thing happens. If I HOLD my card against the reader, it begins to flash blue and then red repeatedly.  This occurs at both doors (north, toward the Loop Road, and south, toward COB).   I was told that the RFID function doesn't work and just to swipe, but I am not certain I can swipe without the RFID detector going off. I have tried, and I think I've been successful, but the same outcome occurred. I spoke with Access Control about this, and they said that the problem needs to be addressed by facilities, so I am trying that.
